nla 0.1.0
  • Reference

Generate a daily temperature matrix based on a modified version of the Logan-Parton model.

Source: R/dailytemps.R
dt_modifiedlp.Rd

Generate a daily temperature matrix based on a modified version of the Logan-Parton model.

Usage

dt_modifiedlp(
  dtr,
  tbar,
  resolution = 1,
  tempdecimalprecision = 1,
  clamp = TRUE
)

Arguments

dtr

Daily temperature range.

tbar

A vector of mean temperatures of the day.

resolution

Temporal resolution of the output (in terms of hours).

tempdecimalprecision

Decimal precision of the resultant temperature.

clamp

Whether to clamp the output values between the minimum and maximum values of tbar.

Value

A matrix of 24/resolution (rows) x length(tbar) (cols) containing temperature values at given times.

Note

If length(tbar) == 1, clamp is automatically set to FALSE.

Modifications

This is a heavily restricted and modified version of the Logan-Parton model. It is parameterised to a day length of 12 and a sunrise time of 6am.

It makes sure that the mean and median of a given column equals the associated tbar

References

Parton, W.J. and Logan, J.A., 1981. A model for diurnal variation in soil and air temperature. Agricultural meteorology, 23, pp.205-216.

Examples

dt_modifiedlp(10, tbar = seq(20,45,0.1))
#>      20 20.1 20.2 20.3 20.4 20.5 20.6 20.7 20.8 20.9   21 21.1 21.2 21.3 21.4
#> 6  20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0
#> 7  20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0
#> 8  20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.0 20.1 20.2 20.3 20.4 20.5 20.6 20.7 20.8 20.9 21.0
#> 9  21.5 21.6 21.7 21.8 21.9 22.0 22.1 22.2 22.3 22.4 22.5 22.6 22.7 22.8 22.9
#> 10 23.0 23.1 23.2 23.3 23.4 23.5 23.6 23.7 23.8 23.9 24.0 24.1 24.2 24.3 24.4
#> 11 24.2 24.3 24.4 24.5 24.6 24.7 24.8 24.9 25.0 25.1 25.2 25.3 25.4 25.5 25.6
#> 12 25.1 25.2 25.3 25.4 25.5 25.6 25.7 25.8 25.9 26.0 26.1 26.2 26.3 26.4 26.5
